behaviors.description: Out-of-stock products are excluded regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is filter->ranker wiring: a high-score item can leak through if availability filtering is applied at the wrong stage. Use an in-process test with catalog module + availability filter + ranking pipeline. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no real AI/ML judgment required by behavior text; single request (not multi-step agent pipeline). Rejected System/Agentic because the behavior text is only about exclusion logic in recommendation results.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test catalog with mixed stock states and scores, run the full recommendation service path, and assert zero out-of-stock IDs appear in returned ranked results.

behaviors.description: Blocked categories in user settings are excluded.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff between user-preference retrieval and catalog filtering, not just inside one pure predicate. Integration-brake: no OS/network/subprocess dependency in behavior; no model-output-quality dependency; no multi-step agent loop. Rejected System/Agentic since behavior text specifies explicit user settings exclusion.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user profile with blocked categories, run recommendation generation through settings lookup + filter + ranking modules, and assert blocked-category products are absent.

behaviors.description: Products matching recent browsing patterns rank above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily ranking behavior, but real regressions often come from feature extraction->scoring handoff, so minimum is module-wired ranking test. Integration-brake: no required real network/OS/subprocess; behavior text does not require non-deterministic LLM/AI API output; single-step ranking request. Rejected Agentic because text describes a local recommendation algorithm, not a real external model call.
behaviors.test_description: Build fixtures with controlled browsing signals, execute feature builder + ranker together, and assert items with browsing matches score/rank above otherwise comparable no-signal items.

behaviors.description: Pagination is correct and stable; same page request twice returns identical results if data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is ranking order + paginator interaction; unit-testing paginator alone can miss instability from upstream ordering keys. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML output dependency; single-step flow. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is deterministic pagination over unchanged data.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations once from fixed fixtures, request page 2 twice through service API, and assert identical ordered IDs and page boundaries.

behaviors.description: At least one recommendation comes from an unbrowsed category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: Likely implemented as diversification logic after base ranking, so failure appears when candidate generation and diversification combine. Integration-brake: no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement; no stated dependence on non-deterministic model call; single-step result assembly. Rejected Agentic because behavior text specifies a product-mix rule, not real model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide user history concentrated in a few categories, run candidate generation + diversification stage, and assert at least one returned item is from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users get popularity-based recommendations, not empty results.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback orchestration: new-user detection must trigger popularity source correctly. Unit tests of either piece alone can miss handoff bugs. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess in behavior statement; no required AI/ML output judgment; no multi-step agentic chain. Rejected System/Agentic based on text: popularity fallback for no-history users.
behaviors.test_description: Create a user with zero history, execute recommendation service end-to-end in-process, and assert non-empty list sourced from popularity ranking path.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records product ID, list position, and timestamp correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is data contract handoff between recommendation response context and tracking persistence. Needs service + tracker + local store together. Integration-brake: real OS/network/subprocess not required (local DB/file is enough); no AI/ML dependency; no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System because behavior does not require external telemetry infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Emit a recommendation list, simulate a click on a known position, persist tracking event to local test DB, and assert recorded product_id, rank_position, and timestamp fields are correct.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: Performance SLA at this scale depends on real runtime characteristics (CPU, memory, indexing, process behavior). In-process mocks can hide true latency. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly sets a wall-clock target ('under 200ms') for large real workload; synthetic stubs are insufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-decision sequence is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Run a repeatable performance test in a real process with a 100k-product dataset, warm up, then measure p95 end-to-end recommendation latency and fail if it exceeds 200ms.

behaviors.description: Purchased recommended products are deprioritized in later requests.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears across event ingestion + user-state update + ranking stages; single-function tests miss stale-state propagation bugs. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior; no explicit non-deterministic AI output dependency; single-step subsequent request check. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is deterministic reprioritization after purchase.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, mark a recommended item as purchased, trigger state update, request recommendations again, and assert that item's rank drops relative to prior response.

behaviors.description: Same user state yields deterministic identical ranked lists across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures usually emerge from interaction of sorting keys, tie-breakers, and state snapshot handling across modules, so integration is minimum. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement stated; no real AI/ML non-deterministic output required by behavior text; single-step repeated call. Rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly expects deterministic output under unchanged state.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/context state, call recommendation endpoint twice in-process with no intervening events, and assert full ordered list equality across both responses.
